data.narrative:
Starcloud is a Space & Aerospace company at SPAC stage, currently valued at $1.1B following $170M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of 7 private companies in the same sector and similar valuation tier, including Impulse Space ($4.3B, SPAC), Apex Space ($2.3B, Series D), Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B, Series B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B, Series C), and Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B).

Peer-median analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. The median valuation across the 7-company peer set is $1.5B, with a range spanning $840M to $4.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as Starcloud's revenue is not on file; while the Space & Aerospace public sector trades at a median 25.8x price-to-sales (across 6 tickers including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL), private companies rarely disclose sufficient revenue data for reliable P/S application.

Based on peer-median analysis alone, the valuation range is $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. Starcloud's current $1.1B valuation sits modestly below this peer median, suggesting modest upside or fair value depending on company-specific unit economics and near-term revenue trajectory. The primary uncertainty is revenue visibility; without disclosed financials, income-based methods remain unavailable. Stage comparability within the peer set also varies meaningfully.
